title: paederia foetida f. microphylla
intro: Paederia foetida f. microphylla, also known as the "stinking passionflower" or "foetid passionflower," is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in treating wounds, skin infections, and various other health conditions. The health benefits of Paederia foetida f. microphylla include reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, Paederia foetida f. microphylla is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is also used in some traditional Chinese medicine formulations.
